differences.title: 1. Rendering
differences.leftDesc: Hydrates only interactive components, shipping zero JS by default.
differences.rightDesc: Renders on server or client, offering flexibility for dynamic apps.

differences.title: 2. Performance
differences.leftDesc: Significantly smaller bundles and faster load times for content sites.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent performance with optimized builds and code splitting.

differences.title: 3. Content Strategy
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, portfolios, and marketing sites prioritizing content delivery.
differences.rightDesc: Best for dynamic applications with complex user interactions.

differences.title: 4. UI Framework Agnostic
differences.leftDesc: Supports React, Vue, Svelte, and others with adapter support.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built around the React ecosystem.

differences.title: 5. JavaScript Shipping
differences.leftDesc: Ships zero JavaScript to the client by default, enhancing performance.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages JavaScript for both server and client rendering.

differences.title: 6. Learning Curve
differences.leftDesc: Easier to grasp for static site generation and basic interactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Steeper curve due to React and full-stack concepts.

differences.title: 7. Use Case
differences.leftDesc: Excellent for marketing, blogs, documentation, and portfolios.
differences.rightDesc: Suitable for e-commerce, SaaS platforms, and complex web apps.
